questions.question: Despite the significant increase in security measures, the frequency of data breaches has not diminished as expected.

options.A: Data breaches have become more frequent even though security protocols were enhanced.
options.B: Security measures have failed to reduce data breaches, which continue to occur at the same rate despite the upgrades.
options.C: The expectation that more security would result in fewer data breaches has not been realized.
options.D: If security measures had not been increased, the number of data breaches would have been much higher than they are now.
options.E: The rise in security protocols is the primary reason why data breaches have finally started to decrease.

explanation.logical_structure: Cümledeki 'Despite' (zıtlık) ve 'not diminished as expected' (beklendiği gibi azalmadı) kısımları anahtar elemanlardır.
explanation.tactic: Adım 1: Ana fikri çıkar (Önlemler arttı ama beklenen düşüş olmadı). Adım 2: 'Beklenti' (expectation) ve 'gerçekleşmemek' (not realized) kelimelerini içeren C şıkkını bul. Adım 3: 'Diminish' (azalmak) kelimesinin karşılığının 'result in fewer' olduğunu doğrula.
explanation.elimination: A şıkkı ihlallerin 'arttığını' söyler (metinde artış değil, beklenen düşüşün olmaması vurgulanır). B şıkkı 'aynı oranda' (same rate) diyerek metinde olmayan bir kesinlik ekler. E şıkkı ise metnin tam tersini söyler.
explanation.trap: Sadece anlama bakmayın; orijinal cümledeki 'beklenti' (expected) vurgusunu taşımayan şıklar eksiktir.

questions.question: Had the researchers known about the potential side effects, they would never have proceeded with the clinical trials.

options.A: Researchers were aware of the side effects, yet they decided to continue the clinical trials regardless.
options.B: The clinical trials were canceled because the researchers discovered the side effects just in time.
options.C: The researchers proceeded with the trials only because they were unaware of the possible adverse effects.
options.D: Even if the side effects had been known, the researchers would have likely continued the trials.
options.E: Since the researchers knew about the side effects, they decided not to start the clinical trials.

explanation.logical_structure: Cümle 'Had the researchers known...' (If they had known) yapısıyla bir Type 3 Conditional (geçmişte gerçekleşmemiş durum) kurar.
explanation.tactic: Adım 1: Koşulu gerçeğe dönüştür (Bilselerdi yapmazlardı -> Demek ki Bilmiyorlardı ve Yaptılar). Adım 2: 'Unaware' (farkında olmayan) ve 'proceeded' (devam etti) kelimelerinin olduğu C şıkkını seç.
explanation.elimination: A, B ve E şıkları araştırmacıların bildiğini veya testin iptal edildiğini söyler; oysa orijinal cümle 'bilselerdi yapmazlardı' diyerek aslında yaptıklarını ima eder.
explanation.trap: Bu sorularda gizli If yapısını (Had they...) fark edememek Type 2 veya Type 1 ile karıştırmaya yol açar.

questions.question: It is not so much the lack of talent as the lack of perseverance that prevents most artists from achieving fame.

options.A: Artists who lack talent will never achieve fame, regardless of how hard they work or persevere.
options.B: Success for artists depends more on their determination than on their inherent abilities.
options.C: Both talent and perseverance are equally necessary for an artist to become famous in their field.
options.D: Many artists are famous not because they are talented, but because they are extremely persistent.
options.E: A lack of talent is the primary reason why most artists fail to gain recognition in the art world.

explanation.logical_structure: 'Not so much X as Y' (X'ten ziyade Y) kalıbı vurguyu Y üzerine koyar.
explanation.tactic: Adım 1: Karşılaştırılan iki şeyi bul (Talent vs Perseverance). Adım 2: Hangisinin daha önemli olduğunu anla (Perseverance yani azim daha önemli). Adım 3: 'Depends more on determination' (azim/kararlılık) diyen B şıkkını seç.
explanation.elimination: C şıkkı her ikisi de eşittir (equally) der (yanlış). E şıkkı yeteneği (talent) öne çıkarır (metne göre yanlış).
explanation.trap: Vurgunun hangi taraf (yetenek mi azim mi) üzerinde olduğunu karıştırmayın; 'as'den sonra gelen kazanır.

questions.question: The use of artificial intelligence in recruitment has raised concerns regarding algorithmic bias and fairness.

options.A: Recruitment is now fairer thanks to the implementation of artificial intelligence and its algorithms.
options.B: AI in hiring processes has sparked worries about potential prejudices and lack of objectivity in algorithms.
options.C: Algorithmic bias is the main reason why companies are now refusing to use AI in their recruitment.
options.D: Concerns about fairness have led to the complete removal of artificial intelligence from modern hiring.
options.E: If AI were used more in recruitment, concerns about algorithmic bias would likely disappear.

explanation.logical_structure: Konu: 'AI in recruitment'. Durum: 'Raised concerns' (endişeleri artırdı).
explanation.tactic: Adım 1: 'Raised concerns' ifadesinin karşılığını bul (Sparked worries). Adım 2: 'Bias' (taraflılık) kelimesinin karşılığını bul (Prejudice). Adım 3: 'Recruitment' (işe alım) kelimesinin karşılığını bul (Hiring). Sonuç: B.
explanation.elimination: A şıkkı olumludur (metne aykırı). C şıkkı 'refusing to use' (kullanmayı reddetme) diyerek aşırıya kaçar. D şıkkı 'complete removal' der, metinde sadece endişe (concern) vardır.
explanation.trap: Raised concerns (endişe yarattı) ifadesi ile 'Refusing to use' (tamamen bırakmak) arasındaki doz farkına dikkat edin.

questions.question: Hardly had the new environmental policy been implemented when several industrial giants filed a lawsuit against the government.

options.A: Industrial giants sued the government just before the new environmental policy was officially enacted.
options.B: The government was sued by large companies immediately after the introduction of the new environmental regulations.
options.C: The new environmental policy was delayed because many industries decided to take legal action.
options.D: Several companies threatened to sue the government if the environmental policy was implemented.
options.E: After the lawsuit was filed, the government decided to implement a new environmental policy.

questions.question: The findings of the study suggest that urban planning must prioritize public transport to alleviate traffic congestion effectively.

options.A: According to the study, traffic jams can only be solved if cities focus on improving their public transportation systems.
options.B: The study concludes that urban planning has failed to address the issue of traffic congestion through public transport.
options.C: Traffic congestion will be solved even if urban planners do not prioritize public transportation systems.
options.D: Urban planning is the main cause of traffic congestion, according to the findings of the recent study.
options.E: Public transport is more important than urban planning when it comes to solving the city's traffic issues.

questions.question: Only by diversifying its energy sources can the country ensure long-term economic stability and energy security.

options.A: The country’s energy security is guaranteed as long as it does not change its current energy sources.
options.B: Diversifying energy sources is one of many ways the country can achieve economic stability.
options.C: The country will achieve economic stability even if it relies on a single source of energy.
options.D: The country cannot have a stable economy and secure energy unless it utilizes a variety of energy types.
options.E: Economic stability is the only reason why the country is trying to diversify its energy sources.

questions.question: The more people rely on digital communication, the less they develop their face-to-face social skills.

options.A: Digital communication is helping people improve their interpersonal skills in real-life situations.
options.B: A decrease in digital communication results in a lack of social skills in modern society.
options.C: Social skills are developed through digital communication just as much as through personal contact.
options.D: There is an inverse relationship between the use of digital tools for talking and the growth of in-person social abilities.
options.E: People should stop using digital communication if they want to maintain their social status.

questions.question: In contrast to her predecessor, the new CEO is far more inclined to consult with the staff before making major decisions.

options.A: The previous CEO used to ask the staff for their opinions more often than the current one does.
options.B: The new CEO prefers to make decisions independently, unlike the person who held the position before.
options.C: Unlike the former CEO, the current one is much more likely to seek staff input for significant choices.
options.D: Major decisions are now made by the staff instead of the CEO, which is a change from the past.
options.E: Both the current and former CEOs have always valued the opinions of their staff members.

questions.question: Hardly any students managed to pass the final exam without attending the supplementary review sessions.

options.A: Most students who attended the review sessions failed the final exam anyway.
options.B: Almost all students who were successful in the final exam had gone to the extra review classes.
options.C: The review sessions were mandatory for all students who wanted to pass the final exam.
options.D: Many students passed the exam despite not attending any of the supplementary sessions.
options.E: The supplementary review sessions were not very helpful for the students who attended them.

questions.question: The extinction of certain species is inevitable unless we take immediate action to preserve their natural habitats.

options.A: Even if we protect their habitats, some species are still going to become extinct eventually.
options.B: The only way to prevent some species from dying out is to act now to save where they live.
options.C: Taking immediate action will not be enough to stop the extinction of most endangered species.
options.D: Natural habitats are being destroyed so fast that species extinction has become unavoidable.
options.E: Preserving natural habitats is a waste of time because species extinction is a natural process.

questions.question: Few people realize that the success of the project was due more to luck than to careful planning.

options.A: It is common knowledge that the project's success was the result of meticulous planning.
options.B: Many people believe that luck played no role in the successful completion of the project.
options.C: The project succeeded because the planning was much better than anybody had anticipated.
options.D: Not many individuals are aware that the project turned out well thanks to fortune rather than strategy.
options.E: Luck and planning were equally responsible for the positive outcome of the project.

questions.question: Provided that the weather remains stable, the construction of the bridge is expected to be finished by the end of the month.

options.A: The bridge will be completed at the end of the month even if the weather conditions change.
options.B: Weather instability might cause the bridge construction to be completed earlier than expected.
options.C: If the weather does not get worse, the bridge should be completed within the current month.
options.D: The construction of the bridge has been delayed until the end of the month due to the weather.
options.E: Since the weather is stable, the bridge will definitely be finished before the month ends.

questions.question: The manager's refusal to listen to his employees' grievances led to a total breakdown in workplace morale.

options.A: Workplace morale improved after the manager finally decided to address the employees' complaints.
options.B: Because the manager ignored the employees' concerns, the atmosphere in the office became very poor.
options.C: The employees stopped complaining because they knew the manager would not listen to them anyway.
options.D: Despite the manager's best efforts to listen, the morale of the workplace continued to decline.
options.E: A lack of morale among the staff was the reason why the manager refused to hear their grievances.

questions.question: While the internet has made information more accessible, it has also facilitated the spread of misinformation.

options.A: The internet is a better source of information than of misinformation, despite what some people think.
options.B: Information is now easier to find, but the internet has also made it easier for false information to circulate.
options.C: Misinformation has become a problem because people no longer use the internet to find facts.
options.D: The accessibility of information on the internet has actually reduced the amount of misinformation.
options.E: If the internet were not so accessible, there would be no misinformation in the world today.

questions.question: Most experts agree that the shift toward remote work is unlikely to be reversed in the near future.

options.A: Experts believe that people will soon return to working in offices as they did before the pandemic.
options.B: There is a consensus among specialists that the trend of working from home is here to stay for a while.
options.C: The shift toward remote work was a mistake that experts hope will be corrected very soon.
options.D: Remote work is becoming less popular according to the majority of recent expert opinions.
options.E: Unless experts change their minds, remote work will remain the only option for all employees.

questions.question: But for the quick intervention of the fire department, the entire building would have been destroyed.

options.A: The fire department arrived too late to save the building from being completely destroyed.
options.B: If the fire department had not acted quickly, the fire would have ruined the whole building.
options.C: The building was saved because the fire department was already present when the fire started.
options.D: Even with the fire department's quick intervention, the building was still heavily damaged.
options.E: The fire department's intervention was not fast enough to prevent the building from burning down.

questions.question: The university's reputation has been tarnished by the recent scandal involving the admission process.

options.A: The admission scandal has actually helped improve the university's public image.
options.B: The university is now more famous because of the scandal related to its entrance procedures.
options.C: The recent scandal concerning admissions has negatively affected the way the university is perceived.
options.D: Despite the admission scandal, the university has managed to keep its excellent reputation.
options.E: The scandal was caused by the university's reputation being too high for many applicants.

questions.question: No sooner had the scientist published his theory than it was challenged by his colleagues in the field.

options.A: The scientist's theory was accepted by his peers as soon as it was published in a journal.
options.B: Before the scientist could publish his theory, his colleagues had already expressed their doubts.
options.C: The theory was challenged by other scientists immediately after it was made public.
options.D: The scientist waited for his colleagues to challenge his theory before he finally published it.
options.E: Many scientists challenged the theory because it was published too early.

questions.question: The economic crisis was so severe that many small businesses were forced to declare bankruptcy.

options.A: Small businesses went bankrupt despite the fact that the economic crisis was not very serious.
options.B: The severity of the economic situation resulted in the closure of numerous small-scale companies.
options.C: Many small businesses managed to survive the economic crisis without filing for bankruptcy.
options.D: If the economic crisis had been more severe, even more small businesses would have failed.
options.E: Bankruptcy was the only way for small businesses to avoid the effects of the economic crisis.

questions.question: Contrary to popular belief, excessive vitamin consumption can be detrimental to one's health.

options.A: Most people correctly believe that taking too many vitamins is bad for their health.
options.B: It is widely thought that vitamins are harmful, but they are actually very beneficial.
options.C: Although many think otherwise, taking an overabundance of vitamins can actually cause harm.
options.D: Vitamins are only healthy if they are consumed in very large quantities every single day.
options.E: Health problems are usually caused by a lack of vitamins rather than an excess of them.

questions.question: Despite the initial setbacks, the research team managed to secure additional funding for the project.

options.A: The project failed to get funding because the research team faced too many initial setbacks.
options.B: Although they encountered early difficulties, the researchers successfully obtained more financial support.
options.C: The research team secured funding even though they did not face any setbacks at the beginning.
options.D: In spite of the funding they secured, the research team still faced many setbacks during the project.
options.E: The team managed to overcome setbacks only after they had secured additional funding for the project.

questions.question: Rarely has the international community seen such a rapid decline in biodiversity as in the last decade.

options.A: Biodiversity has been declining rapidly for many decades according to the international community.
options.B: The international community has seen many rapid declines in biodiversity before the last decade.
options.C: The decrease in biodiversity over the past ten years is among the fastest the world has ever witnessed.
options.D: The international community is not aware of the rapid decline in biodiversity that occurred ten years ago.
options.E: Biodiversity declined more rapidly in the past than it has during the most recent decade.

questions.question: It is widely believed that the transition to renewable energy is essential for mitigating the effects of climate change.

options.A: Many people think that climate change can only be stopped by avoiding renewable energy sources.
options.B: The effects of climate change are widely believed to be the cause of the shift toward renewable energy.
options.C: Switching to green energy is commonly seen as a necessary step in reducing the impact of global warming.
options.D: Essential climate change mitigation is what leads to the widespread belief in renewable energy.
options.E: There is a common belief that renewable energy has little to no effect on the progress of climate change.

questions.question: Had the government implemented stricter regulations earlier, the economic crisis might have been less severe.

options.A: The government implemented strict regulations, but the economic crisis was still quite severe.
options.B: The economic crisis was not severe because the government implemented regulations on time.
options.C: Because the government failed to act sooner with tighter rules, the financial downturn was likely worse.
options.D: The severity of the economic crisis led the government to implement stricter regulations eventually.
options.E: If the economic crisis had been less severe, the government would not have needed to implement regulations.

questions.question: Urbanization often leads to the loss of agricultural land, which in turn affects food security.

options.A: Food security is the primary reason why urbanization results in the loss of farming areas.
options.B: The expansion of cities frequently results in reduced farming space, consequently impacting the food supply.
options.C: When food security is threatened, people tend to move to urban areas and abandon their farms.
options.D: Agricultural land loss is the only factor that determines the food security of an urban population.
options.E: Urbanization and food security are unrelated processes that happen to occur at the same time.

questions.question: The discovery of the new variant prompted health officials to reconsider their existing safety protocols.

options.A: Health officials ignored the new variant and kept their original safety protocols in place.
options.B: Existing safety protocols were so effective that the new variant was discovered immediately.
options.C: The identification of a new strain caused medical authorities to re-evaluate current safety measures.
options.D: Health officials were already reconsidering their protocols before the new variant was even found.
options.E: The new variant was discovered because health officials had changed their safety protocols.

questions.question: No sooner had the company launched its product than its main competitor announced a similar innovation.

options.A: The competitor announced their innovation just before the company could launch its product.
options.B: The company launched its product immediately after the competitor made their announcement.
options.C: Immediately after the product's release, the primary rival company publicized a comparable invention.
options.D: Both companies announced their innovations at exactly the same time to avoid competition.
options.E: The company decided not to launch the product because the competitor had a better innovation.

questions.question: The study suggests that there is a significant correlation between sleep deprivation and cognitive impairment.

options.A: According to the research, a lack of sleep is strongly linked to a decline in mental processing abilities.
options.B: The study shows that cognitive impairment is what causes most people to suffer from sleep deprivation.
options.C: There is no real evidence in the study to support a link between sleeping habits and brain function.
options.D: Cognitive impairment can be cured by ensuring that sleep deprivation is avoided at all costs.
options.E: The study implies that people with high cognitive abilities need significantly less sleep than others.

questions.question: Unless the demand for electric vehicles increases, manufacturers are unlikely to lower their prices.

options.A: Manufacturers will lower their prices even if the demand for electric vehicles remains low.
options.B: Electric vehicle prices will probably stay the same if there isn't a rise in consumer interest.
options.C: High prices are the reason why there is such a low demand for electric vehicles today.
options.D: As soon as manufacturers lower their prices, the demand for electric vehicles will increase.
options.E: The lack of demand is caused by the fact that manufacturers refuse to lower their prices.

questions.question: The witness's testimony was inconsistent with the evidence found at the crime scene.

options.A: What the witness said in court matched the physical evidence discovered by the police.
options.B: The evidence at the crime scene was found because of the witness's consistent testimony.
options.C: The account provided by the witness did not align with the facts gathered from the scene.
options.D: The witness refused to give testimony because the evidence at the crime scene was too confusing.
options.E: In spite of the evidence at the crime scene, the witness gave a very convincing testimony.

questions.question: Despite being the most qualified candidate, he was not offered the executive position.

options.A: He was offered the executive position because he was more qualified than anyone else.
options.B: Even though he had the best qualifications, the company did not give him the job.
options.C: The executive position was given to him because his qualifications were simply unmatched.
options.D: He turned down the executive position although he was the most suitable candidate for it.
options.E: Since he was not the most qualified candidate, he was not surprised when he didn't get the job.

questions.question: The complexity of the tax system makes it difficult for small business owners to remain compliant.

options.A: Small business owners find it easy to follow tax laws despite the complexity of the system.
options.B: If the tax system were more complex, small business owners would find it easier to comply.
options.C: The intricate nature of tax regulations poses a challenge for small businesses to follow the law.
options.D: Remaining compliant is what makes the tax system so complex for small business owners.
options.E: Small business owners often ignore the tax system because it is too simple for them.

questions.question: The results of the experiment were so inconclusive that a second trial had to be conducted.

options.A: The first trial provided such clear data that there was no need for a second experiment.
options.B: Because the initial findings lacked clarity, the researchers were required to repeat the test.
options.C: A second trial was conducted to confirm the very successful results of the first experiment.
options.D: The experiment was concluded after the first trial because the results were so definitive.
options.E: Researchers conducted a second trial even though the results of the first were conclusive.

questions.question: Due to the rise of remote work, many corporations are downsizing their physical office spaces.

options.A: Corporations are buying larger offices because more employees are working from home.
options.B: Remote work is decreasing, so companies are forced to expand their office buildings.
options.C: The trend of working from home has led many firms to reduce the size of their offices.
options.D: Many corporations are moving to remote work because they cannot afford their current office space.
options.E: Downsizing office spaces is what originally caused the rise in remote work opportunities.

questions.question: Few people realize the extent to which plastic pollution affects marine ecosystems.

options.A: The majority of people are well aware of how much plastic harms life in the ocean.
options.B: The impact of plastic waste on sea life is not understood by a large portion of the population.
options.C: Plastic pollution only affects a small part of the marine ecosystem, as most people know.
options.D: It is common knowledge that plastic pollution has a devastating effect on the oceans.
options.E: People are starting to realize that marine ecosystems are actually improving despite plastic.

questions.question: The curriculum was revised with a view to incorporating more practical skills for the students.

options.A: The curriculum was changed so that students could focus less on practical skills.
options.B: In order to include more hands-on training, the educational program underwent revisions.
options.C: Practical skills were removed from the curriculum during the most recent revision process.
options.D: Students requested a revised curriculum because they already had enough practical skills.
options.E: The revision of the curriculum made it harder for students to learn any practical skills.

questions.question: Regardless of the outcome of the election, the country is likely to face significant social unrest.

options.A: The election results will determine whether or not the country faces social unrest.
options.B: Social unrest can be avoided if the election results are favorable to the public.
options.C: No matter who wins the vote, the nation will probably experience substantial public disorder.
options.D: The country is facing social unrest because the outcome of the election is already known.
options.E: Significant social unrest is the only thing that could change the outcome of the election.

questions.question: The professor's lecture was so profound that it left the students questioning their own assumptions.

options.A: The students already questioned their assumptions before they heard the professor's lecture.
options.B: The lecture was so simple that no one in the class felt the need to ask any questions.
options.C: The deep insights of the professor's talk made the students re-examine their beliefs.
options.D: The students' assumptions were confirmed by the profound nature of the professor's lecture.
options.E: Because the lecture was so confusing, the students started to question the professor's knowledge.

questions.question: The new law is intended to prevent companies from exploiting loopholes in the current tax code.

options.A: Companies are encouraged by the new law to find and use loopholes in the tax system.
options.B: The purpose of the recent legislation is to stop firms from taking advantage of tax gaps.
options.C: The current tax code is so perfect that the new law is actually unnecessary for companies.
options.D: Exploiting loopholes is now legal for all companies under the regulations of the new law.
options.E: The new law failed to prevent companies from using the loopholes they found in the tax code.
